let unverifiableInHeadless: [String]; let knownLimitations: [String] }","owning_lane":"polish_resilience","notes":"For final review/reporting so build verification and final review use the same vocabulary."}]}
```

**Claude (Frontend Engineer · User Advocate) — Round 1**

Coming at this as the person who has to make the checklist screen feel instant at 9pm the night before a trip, and who has to make sure three parallel build lanes don't invent three different shapes for the same `Trip` type.

Architecturally I'd keep this to four layers and refuse to let anything cross them informally. Domain (SwiftData `@Model` types plus the pure generation engine, no SwiftUI or StoreKit imports anywhere in this layer), Services (EntitlementStore, NotificationScheduler, WidgetSnapshotPublisher, DeepLinkRouter — each behind a protocol, each injected via `@Environment` rather than singletons reached for ad hoc), Features (one folder per screen, each with its own `@Observable` store that owns a `ModelContext` reference and talks to Services through their protocols, never touching StoreKit or UserNotifications directly), and Shared (design tokens, reusable components, extensions). The one thing I want every parallel builder to internalize before they write a line: the generation engine is a plain synchronous function over value types — `GenerationEngine.generate(input: GenerationInput) -> GenerationResult` — with zero `async`, zero actor-hopping, because it's small, deterministic, local data. If I see anyone wrap that call in a `Task` or slap a loading spinner on Trip Detail for the golden path, that's a bug against the product's own trust story: this list is supposed to feel instant, not "generating." The only place a "generating" state is legitimate is the explicit corrupted/missing-derived-data recovery path design already carved out — that has to be a distinct code path, not the default rendering of a slow computation.

On navigation and state, three independent `NavigationPath`s live on a `TabRouter` (`@Observable`, app-root-scoped), one per tab, and a single `pendingNavigationIntent: PendingNavigationIntent?` also lives at app root, resolved once after launch settles via a `.task` on `AppRootView`. The once-only single-upcoming-trip shortcut is a boolean flag set at cold launch, never re-checked on tab re-tap — I'd make this a `hasFiredColdLaunchShortcut: Bool` living only in memory (not persisted), since the rule is explicitly "once per cold launch," not "once ever." `DeepLinkRouter.resolve(_:)` has to return an enum result (`.route(TabRoute)` or `.tripNotFound`), and `.tripNotFound` drives the exact same `ToastCenter` used for the changed-list moment — one component, two copy variants, and I want that literally enforced by both call sites using the same `ToastMessage` type so nobody invents a second toast implementation under time pressure.

For SwiftData itself: `Trip`, `PackingItem`, `Review`, `ReviewItemOutcome` as `@Model` types, one `VersionedSchema` (`SchemaV1`) defined from the first commit. Trip has a `.cascade` delete rule into its `PackingItem`s and `Review`; `didHappen: Bool` (default `true`) is the soft-exclude flag, never a delete — and I want a concrete implementation note here: the learning-history query (`LearningHistoryReader`) filters `didHappen == true` at the query level, so a trip marked "didn't happen" structurally cannot leak into the generation engine's input even if someone forgets to check the flag somewhere else. Regeneration is a full replace-and-diff of the *suggested* item subset only: snapshot the current suggested items' names and checked state before regenerating, run the pure engine, then for each new suggested item, if its name matches an old suggested item with an unchanged reason, keep the old checked state and `isRecentlyChanged = false`; otherwise it's new or its reason changed, so `isRecentlyChanged = true`. Items in the "Added by you" section are never touched by this pass — they're a completely separate model relationship, not a flag on the same table, so there's no way for regeneration logic to accidentally sweep up a custom item.

The lock-status property is the one place I want to be pedantic about performance, because it's exactly the kind of thing that looks fine with three trips in the simulator and jitters in production with fifty. `Trip` needs zero relationship access to compute lock status — `isWithinFreeRetentionWindow(tripDate:allTripDates:)` takes only an array of dates (id + date projection, not full objects) and the trip's own date. `TripCard`'s view body must never reference `trip.packingItems` or `trip.review` — only `TripDetailView` is allowed to fault those in. I'd enforce this by literally not exposing those relationships on whatever lightweight type `TripListView`'s `@Query` decodes into for the card row, forcing a second, explicit fetch when someone actually opens a trip.

One thing I want to push back on preemptively, because it's a trap the "common pitfalls" pattern language invites: destination search is over a bundled ~30-40 entry in-memory array, not a network call. Do not debounce it, do not wrap it in `.task(id:)` with a sleep, do not show a loading state — that's the correct pattern for remote search and the wrong pattern here; it would add perceptible lag to something that should be synchronous filtering on every keystroke. Climate data itself loads once at app launch into an in-memory `ClimateCatalog` singleton-ish object behind a protocol, never touched again from disk after that.

Widgets and Live Activities: per the architecture decision, they never see SwiftData. `WidgetSnapshot` (days-until-trip, percent-complete, stable trip id) is a small `Codable` struct that has to physically exist as one file with dual target membership (main app + widget extension target) — not a separate framework, that's overkill for one struct, and not two hand-copied definitions in two targets, which is exactly how these drift and someone ships a widget reading stale field names. `AppIntents` for the Siri add-item path live in the main app target, not the widget extension — intents run by briefly waking the host app and can call domain services directly, so they don't need the App Group indirection that's specifically a WidgetKit/ActivityKit cross-process constraint.

For testing, my honest contribution beyond what architecture already locked: the two XCUITest critical paths need to run against an in-memory `ModelContainer` seeded via launch-argument fixtures, not the real on-disk store — otherwise these tests are slow and occasionally flaky for reasons that have nothing to do with the product. Store-shape unit tests belong in Swift Testing and should include the regeneration diff algorithm specifically (given an old suggested set and a new engine output, assert exactly which items get `isRecentlyChanged` and which retain checked state) since that's genuinely fiddly logic that's easy to get subtly wrong and invisible to eyeball-test.

The single detail I most want to survive untouched into build: `GenerationEngine.generate` is a plain synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated pure function — the moment someone makes it `async` "to be safe," they've reopened the door to a spinner appearing on the one screen that has to feel instant.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "Trip", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class Trip { var id: UUID; var destinationDisplayName: String; var regionId: String?; var climateZoneFallbackId: String?; var startDate: Date; var endDate: Date; var tripType: TripType; var carryOnOnly: Bool; var laundryAccess: Bool; var didHappen: Bool; var createdAt: Date; var lastGeneratedAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [PackingItem]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var review: Review? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "didHappen defaults true; false is the soft-exclude flag, never a delete. lastGeneratedAt distinguishes normal vs 'generating'-recovery state."},
  {"name": "PackingItem", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class PackingItem { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var name: String; var category: PackingCategory; var isCustom: Bool; var isChecked: Bool; var quantity: Int; var suggestionReason: String?; var isRecentlyChanged: Bool; var sortOrder: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "suggestionReason/isRecentlyChanged frozen at generation/regeneration time only, never recomputed at render. isCustom items are excluded from all regeneration diffing."},
  {"name": "Review", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class Review { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var completedAt: Date; var wishIPacked: String?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var itemOutcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Editable/reopenable; edits trigger full deterministic recomputation of downstream aggregates via LearningHistoryReader, never incremental updates."},
  {"name": "ReviewItemOutcome", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class ReviewItemOutcome { var id: UUID; var review: Review?; var itemName: String; var category: PackingCategory; var wasUsed: Bool }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Only source of learning signal. Checklist-checking during packing never writes here."},
  {"name": "TripType", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum TripType: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case business, cityWeekend, beachWarm, coldWinter, outdoorHiking, familyKids }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Closed six-item taxonomy, locked in design phase."},
  {"name": "PackingCategory", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PackingCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case clothing, toiletries, electronics, documents, health, gear }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Closed set for checklist section grouping; custom items still get a category for sectioning but no user-facing taxonomy editing."},
  {"name": "ClimateNormal", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ClimateNormal: Codable { let regionOrZoneId: String; let month: Int; let averageHighC: Double; let averageLowC: Double; let averagePrecipitationMM: Double; let notes: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Bundled JSON, decoded once at launch. UI copy must always read 'historical average', never forecast language."},
  {"name": "ClimateCatalog", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ClimateCatalog { func normal(for regionOrZoneId: String, month: Int) -> ClimateNormal?; func allRegions() -> [RegionCatalogEntry]; func allFallbackZones() -> [ClimateZoneFallbackEntry] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Loaded once in-memory; a CI test asserts every bundled region/zone has all 12 months present."},
  {"name": "RegionCatalogEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RegionCatalogEntry: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let displayName: String; let climateZoneFallbackId: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "~30-40 bundled destinations."},
  {"name": "ClimateZoneFallbackEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ClimateZoneFallbackEntry: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let displayName: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Manual fallback bucket shown inline the instant destination search returns zero matches."},
  {"name": "monthOfMajorityDays", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func monthOfMajorityDays(start: Date, end: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> Int", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Deterministic month-boundary resolution for climate lookup."},
  {"name": "GenerationInput", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GenerationInput { let tripType: TripType; let month: Int; let regionOrZoneId: String; let tripLengthDays: Int; let carryOnOnly: Bool; let laundryAccess: Bool; let climate: [ClimateNormal]; let learningHistory: LearningHistorySnapshot }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fully snapshotted value type; nothing here is a live SwiftData reference."},
  {"name": "GenerationResult", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GenerationResult { struct SuggestedItem { let name: String; let category: PackingCategory; let quantity: Int; let reason: String }; let items: [SuggestedItem] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure output; caller diffs against prior suggested-item snapshot to set isRecentlyChanged."},
  {"name": "GenerationEngine", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol GenerationEngine { func generate(input: GenerationInput) -> GenerationResult }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "MUST stay synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated. This is the one rule that keeps trip creation feeling instant."},
  {"name": "LearningHistorySnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct LearningHistorySnapshot { struct ItemEvidence { let itemName: String; let category: PackingCategory; let usedCount: Int; let unusedCount: Int; let lastSeenDate: Date }; let evidenceByItem: [ItemEvidence]; let minimumEvidenceCount: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "minimumEvidenceCount = 2 (same-type-or-region data points) before a suggestion visibly shifts, per detailed_discussion."},
  {"name": "LearningHistoryReader", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol LearningHistoryReader { func snapshot(for tripType: TripType, regionOrZoneId: String, context: ModelContext) -> LearningHistorySnapshot }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Always recomputes fresh from raw ReviewItemOutcome + didHappen==true trips; never an incremental cache."},
  {"name": "isWithinFreeRetentionWindow", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func isWithinFreeRetentionWindow(tripId: UUID, tripDate: Date, allTripDatesById: [UUID: Date], isPaidEntitled: Bool) -> Bool", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Cheap, relationship-free; must never fault in items/review to compute."},
  {"name": "TabRoute", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum TabRoute { case tripList, tripDetail(UUID), review(UUID), insights, settings, paywall(PaywallContext) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Used as NavigationPath element per-tab; each tab owns its own path array."},
  {"name": "PendingNavigationIntent", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PendingNavigationIntent { case openTrip(UUID); case openReview(UUID); case openPaywall }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Single app-root value resolved once per external trigger via DeepLinkRouter, preventing race between simultaneous triggers."},
  {"name": "DeepLinkRouter", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol DeepLinkRouter { func resolve(_ intent: PendingNavigationIntent, context: ModelContext) -> DeepLinkResolution }; enum DeepLinkResolution { case route(TabRoute); case tripNotFound }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "tripNotFound drives the same ToastCenter used for the changed-list moment, different copy only."},
  {"name": "ToastCenter", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable final class ToastCenter { func show(_ message: ToastMessage) }; struct ToastMessage { let text: String; let style: ToastStyle } ", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "One shared component for changed-list moment and tripNotFound fallback."},
  {"name": "ColorStory", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ColorStory: CaseIterable { case hotArid, tropicalHumid, temperateMild, coldContinental, alpine, mediterranean; func hexPair() -> (primary: String, secondary: String); func textColor(onLuminance: Double) -> Color }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Pure function of climate zone + season; formula-driven contrast, never hand-picked."},
  {"name": "WidgetSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct WidgetSnapshot: Codable { let stableTripId: UUID; let daysUntilTrip: Int; let percentComplete: Double; let hasPendingReview: Bool }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "One file, dual target membership (main app + widget extension). Offline grace window biases toward preserving unlocked access."},

Never touches isCustom items."},
  {"name": "AccessibilityCollapseAdapter", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func collapsedHeaderStyle(colorStory: ColorStory, dynamicTypeSize: DynamicTypeSize, increaseContrast: Bool, reduceTransparency: Bool) -> HeaderRenderStyle", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "One shared 'collapse to flat/simple' mechanism reused across Dynamic Type XXL, Increase Contrast, and Reduce Transparency."},

**Gemini — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Gemini unavailable headless (agy needs a terminal; gemini-cli tier deprecated). gemini(key) -> Warning: 256-color support not detected. Using a terminal with at least 256-color support is recomme | gemini CLI -> auth/tier error_

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

Looking at round 1: Codex and Claude both did real architecture work, and they agree on almost every non-functional rule — cascade-delete on trip deletion, `didHappen` as a soft-exclude flag that's structurally invisible to the learning engine (not just a convention), deterministic full recompute on review edits (never incremental), a minimum-evidence threshold before suggestions visibly shift, the cheap relationship-free free-tier lock check, App-Group-only widget snapshots with no destination text, zero LLM surface, and the same lean test pyramid (Swift Testing for pure logic, two XCUITest critical paths, a real climate-bundle completeness test). Nobody is fighting about any of that.

But there's a real, unresolved fork underneath: Codex modeled persistence as a **value-type generated artifact** (`PackingListArtifact`/`PackingItemArtifact`) written through a `GeneratedListRepository`, with review data as an immutable `ReviewOutcomeSnapshot`/`ReviewedItemOutcome` log rebuilt into `HistorySignalSnapshot`s by a `LearningHistoryBuilding` service. Claude modeled persistence as **SwiftData `@Model` classes directly** (`Trip`, `PackingItem` with `suggestionReason`/`isRecentlyChanged` fields living right on the row, `Review`, `ReviewItemOutcome` with `wasUsed: Bool`). These aren't just naming differences — they're two different shapes for where "frozen reason string" and "review outcome" actually live, and if parallel build lanes pick different ones, the checklist UI, the generation engine, and the review flow will disagree about what type they're handed. This is exactly the kind of conflict the phase's acceptance check exists to catch.

CONSENSUS: NO

Next round needs Codex and Claude to pick **one** canonical shape: either (a) SwiftData `@Model` rows carry the frozen reason/changed fields directly (Claude's version — simpler, one less mapping layer), or (b) a separate immutable value-type artifact is the thing that's persisted and the `@Model` layer is just its storage container (Codex's version — cleaner separation between domain snapshot and storage). They also need to converge on one review-outcome shape (`ItemUsageOutcome` enum with `wishedPacked` vs. a plain `wasUsed: Bool` + separate free-text field) and one name for the generation engine's input/output types so every lane codes against the same contract.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "TripType", "kind": "enum", "language": "Swift", "signature": "enum TripType: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case business, cityWeekend, beachWarm, coldWinter, outdoorHiking, familyKids }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Agreed by both contributions; closed six-item taxonomy."},
  {"name": "TripStatus/didHappen", "kind": "enum", "language": "Swift", "signature": "OPEN: Codex proposes `enum TripStatus { planned, completed, didNotHappen }` on a value-type trip snapshot; Claude proposes `var didHappen: Bool` directly on the @Model Trip. Pick one.", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Both agree the semantics (soft-exclude, structurally filtered out of learning queries, never a delete) — only the representation differs. Must resolve next round."},

Pick one.", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Both agree reason+changed-flag must be frozen at generation/regeneration time and never live-recomputed; only where that frozen data physically lives differs."},
  {"name": "ReviewOutcome shape", "kind": "struct|class", "language": "Swift", "signature": "OPEN: Codex's ReviewOutcomeSnapshot/ReviewedItemOutcome + ItemUsageOutcome enum (used/unused/wishedPacked) vs Claude's Review/ReviewItemOutcome (@Model, wasUsed: Bool) + separate wishIPacked: String? field on Review. Pick one.", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Functionally equivalent; needs one canonical shape so review UI and learning-history builder agree on a type."},
  {"name": "GenerationEngine contract", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "OPEN: Codex's `PackingListGenerating.generate(trip:climate:history:previous:) -> PackingListArtifact` vs Claude's `GenerationEngine.generate(input: GenerationInput) -> GenerationResult`. Both agree: pure, synchronous, non-async, zero SwiftData/UI dependency, zero LLM.", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Naming/shape must converge; the 'must stay synchronous' rule is already locked and non-negotiable."},
  {"name": "ClimateNormal/ClimateSummary", "kind": "struct", "language": "Swift", "signature": "OPEN: Codex's ClimateSummary (regionKey, month, averageHighC/LowC, PrecipitationBand, copyLabel) vs Claude's ClimateNormal (regionOrZoneId, month, averageHighC/LowC, averagePrecipitationMM, notes) + ClimateCatalog protocol. Similar shape, different field names/precision.", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Both agree: bundled JSON, loaded once in-memory, CI test asserts 12-month completeness per region/fallback zone, UI copy always 'historical average' never forecast."},
  {"name": "EntitlementStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol EntitlementStoreProtocol { var current: EntitlementSnapshot { get }; func start() async; func refresh() async } / struct EntitlementSnapshot { isPremiumActive/isPaid: Bool; activeProductID/lastVerifiedAt; graceWindowEndsAt/isOfflineGracePeriod }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Both agree: long-lived Transaction.updates listener, never one-shot launch check, offline grace window biases toward preserving unlocked access. Field naming needs a single pass next round."},
  {"name": "isWithinFreeRetentionWindow / HistoryAccessPolicy", "kind": "function", "language": "Swift", "signature": "func isWithinFreeRetentionWindow(tripId: UUID, tripDate: Date, allTripDatesById: [UUID: Date], isPaidEntitled: Bool) -> Bool", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Both agree this must be relationship-free and take only id+date projections plus entitlement state, never fault in items/review."},
  {"name": "WidgetSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "Swift", "signature": "struct WidgetSnapshot: Codable, Equatable { let stableTripId/nextTripID: UUID?; let daysUntilTrip: Int?; let percentComplete/packingProgress: Double?; let hasPendingReview: Bool; updatedAt: Date? }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Both agree: no destination text ever, dual target membership as one file (not a framework), debounced publish + WidgetCenter.reloadTimelines."},
  {"name": "WidgetSnapshotPublishing", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol WidgetSnapshotPublishing { func publish(_ snapshot: WidgetSnapshot) async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Agreed by both."},
  {"name": "PendingNavigationIntent / DeepLinkRouter", "kind": "enum|protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "enum PendingNavigationIntent { case openTrip(UUID); case openReview(UUID); case openSettings/openPaywall; case none }; protocol DeepLinkRouter { func resolve(_ intent: PendingNavigationIntent, context: ModelContext) -> DeepLinkResolution }; enum DeepLinkResolution { case route(TabRoute); case tripNotFound }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Agreed by both: single app-root value, tripNotFound drives the same ToastCenter used for the changed-list moment."},

  {"name": "BuildVerificationChecklist", "kind": "struct", "language": "Swift", "signature": "struct BuildVerificationChecklist { let verifiedCoreFlows: [String]; let unverifiableInHeadless: [String]; let knownLimitations: [String] }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Only from Codex's turn but uncontested; shared vocabulary for build verification and final review to agree on what's actually done vs. flagged as a limitation."},

  {"name": "PersistenceBootstrap", "kind": "function", "language": "Swift", "signature": "func makeModelContainer() throws -> ModelContainer", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Only from Claude's turn but uncontested; VersionedSchema (SchemaV1) from first commit, failure surfaces full-bleed error state, never a crash."}
]}
```

### Round 2


**Codex (QA & Risk · Pragmatist) — Round 2**

Claude, the unresolved fork is real, and I don’t think we should pretend it’s just naming. If three build lanes leave this round with two different mental models for “the thing a checklist row is,” we will ship drift: one lane mutates a value-type artifact, another mutates `@Model` rows, and suddenly regeneration, review save, and UI diffing all disagree about ownership. From a QA/risk perspective, the safer answer is Claude’s persistence shape with one important constraint borrowed from Codex: persist the frozen generation facts directly on SwiftData rows, but keep the engine itself entirely value-type and pure. In other words, `Trip`, `PackingItem`, `Review`, and `ReviewItemOutcome` are the canonical stored model, because that removes an otherwise pointless storage-mapping layer for an MVP. But the generator never sees those classes directly. It snapshots them into `GenerationInput`, returns `GenerationResult`, and a coordinator applies that result back onto `PackingItem` rows in one commit. That gets us the auditable “frozen reason string / frozen changed flag” contract Codex cares about, without inventing a second persisted artifact type that every screen then has to translate in and out of just to toggle a checkbox.

Same answer on review shape: keep Claude’s simpler model. `Review` gets `wishIPacked: String?`; each `ReviewItemOutcome` has `wasUsed: Bool`; and “didn’t happen” stays on `Trip` as a trip-level status, not jammed into item outcome semantics. Codex’s richer enum shape is elegant on paper, but for this app it spreads one screen’s special-case action across the item model for no real gain. The post-trip screen has exactly three truths that matter to learning: the trip happened or it didn’t, an item was used or unused, and there may be one free-text “wish I’d packed.” That’s enough. Fewer representational states means fewer invalid combinations to test. Where I do agree with Codex strongly is that we need one orchestration surface and one rebuild story: review edits must force deterministic recompute from raw `ReviewItemOutcome` history, never incremental patching. That is the bug farm otherwise.

So I’d lock the canonical contract like this: SwiftData `@Model` storage is canonical; generation and policy logic operate on structs only; `PackingCoordinator` is the only type allowed to bridge between the two; `GenerationEngine.generate` stays synchronous and non-async; regeneration only rewrites suggested rows and never touches custom rows; lock-state stays relationship-free; widget data is a tiny denormalized snapshot only; and verification explicitly includes a rebuild-all-derived-state path because that’s how we catch schema changes, review edits, and corrupted derived rows without lying to ourselves about recovery. That’s the smallest architecture that actually ships and is still testable.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces":[
  {"name":"Trip","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"@Model final class Trip { var id: UUID; var destinationDisplayName: String; var regionOrZoneId: String?; var climateZoneFallbackId: String?; var startDate: Date; var endDate: Date; var tripTypeRaw: String; var carryOnOnly: Bool; var laundryAccess: Bool; var statusRaw: String; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date; var lastGeneratedAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [PackingItem]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var review: Review? }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Canonical persisted trip model. statusRaw maps to TripStatus. Soft-exclude for learning uses status == .didNotHappen, never delete."},
  {"name":"TripStatus","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum TripStatus: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case planned, completed, didNotHappen }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Preferred over bare didHappen Bool because it closes invalid future migrations and keeps the cancellation path explicit."},
  {"name":"TripType","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum TripType: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case business, cityWeekend, beachWarm, coldWinter, outdoorHiking, familyKids }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Closed six-item taxonomy agreed earlier."},
  {"name":"PackingCategory","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum PackingCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case clothing, toiletries, electronics, documents, health, gear }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Closed section grouping set; no user-editable taxonomy."},
  {"name":"PackingItem","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"@Model final class PackingItem { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var name: String; var categoryRaw: String; var isCustom: Bool; var isChecked: Bool; var quantity: Int; var suggestionReason: String?; var isRecentlyChanged: Bool; var sortOrder: Int }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Canonical persisted checklist row. suggestionReason and isRecentlyChanged are frozen generation-time facts, never recomputed in UI."},
  {"name":"Review","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"@Model final class Review { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var completedAt: Date; var wishIPacked: String?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var itemOutcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome] }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Editable; edits trigger deterministic rebuild from raw outcomes, never incremental patching."},
  {"name":"ReviewItemOutcome","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"@Model final class ReviewItemOutcome { var id: UUID; var review: Review?; var itemName: String; var categoryRaw: String; var wasUsed: Bool }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Only learning signal at item level. Packing checkmarks never write here."},
  {"name":"TripDraft","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct TripDraft: Equatable { let tripID: UUID?; let destinationKey: String; let fallbackClimateZoneKey: String?; let startDate: Date; let endDate: Date; let tripType: TripType; let modifiers: TripModifiers }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Shared create/edit payload across screens and coordinator."},
  {"name":"TripModifiers","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct TripModifiers: Equatable { let carryOnOnly: Bool; let laundryAccess: Bool }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Small stable input surface for generation rules."},
  {"name":"TripSummary","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct TripSummary: Identifiable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let destinationDisplayName: String; let dateRangeText: String; let tripType: TripType; let progress: Double; let isLockedByFreeTier: Bool; let status: TripStatus }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Cheap list-facing projection. Must be computable without faulting item/review graphs."},
  {"name":"ClimateNormal","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct ClimateNormal: Codable, Equatable { let regionOrZoneId: String; let month: Int; let averageHighC: Double; let averageLowC: Double; let averagePrecipitationMM: Double; let notes: String? }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Bundled JSON row. UI copy always labels this as historical average, never forecast."},
  {"name":"RegionCatalogEntry","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct RegionCatalogEntry: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: String; let displayName: String; let climateZoneFallbackId: String }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Bundled offline destination catalog."},
  {"name":"ClimateZoneFallbackEntry","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct ClimateZoneFallbackEntry: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: String; let displayName: String }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Inline fallback bucket when destination search has no match."},
  {"name":"ClimateCatalog","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol ClimateCatalog { func normal(for regionOrZoneId: String, month: Int) -> ClimateNormal?; func allRegions() -> [RegionCatalogEntry]; func allFallbackZones() -> [ClimateZoneFallbackEntry] }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Loaded once into memory; no async search or disk churn in the hot path."},
  {"name":"monthOfMajorityDays","kind":"function","language":"Swift","signature":"func monthOfMajorityDays(start: Date, end: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> Int","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Deterministic month-boundary resolution for climate lookup."},
  {"name":"LearningHistorySnapshot","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct LearningHistorySnapshot: Equatable { struct ItemEvidence: Equatable { let itemName: String; let category: PackingCategory; let usedCount: Int; let unusedCount: Int; let lastSeenDate: Date }; let evidenceByItem: [ItemEvidence]; let minimumEvidenceCount: Int }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Pure generation input distilled from reviewed trips only; minimumEvidenceCount stays 2."},
  {"name":"LearningHistoryReader","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol LearningHistoryReader { func snapshot(for tripType: TripType, regionOrZoneId: String, context: ModelContext) throws -> LearningHistorySnapshot }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Reads raw ReviewItemOutcome history from canonical store; filters out trips with status == .didNotHappen."},
  {"name":"GenerationInput","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct GenerationInput: Equatable { let tripType: TripType; let month: Int; let regionOrZoneId: String; let tripLengthDays: Int; let carryOnOnly: Bool; let laundryAccess: Bool; let climate: ClimateNormal?; let learningHistory: LearningHistorySnapshot }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Pure value-type snapshot passed into generator. Never contains live @Model references."},
  {"name":"GenerationResult","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct GenerationResult: Equatable { struct SuggestedItem: Equatable { let name: String; let category: PackingCategory; let quantity: Int; let reason: String }; let items: [SuggestedItem] }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Pure synchronous output. Coordinator diffs this against prior suggested rows to set changed flags and preserve checked state where allowed."},
  {"name":"GenerationEngine","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol GenerationEngine { func generate(input: GenerationInput) -> GenerationResult }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Non-async, non-actor-isolated, deterministic. This must stay instant for the golden path."},
  {"name":"regenerateSuggestedItems","kind":"function","language":"Swift","signature":"func regenerateSuggestedItems(existing: [PackingItem], result: GenerationResult) -> [PackingItem]","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Rewrites suggested rows only. Keeps checked state when name and reason are unchanged; never touches custom rows."},
  {"name":"TripRepository","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol TripRepository { func fetchSummaries() throws -> [TripSummary]; func fetchTrip(id: UUID) throws -> Trip; func upsert(_ draft: TripDraft) throws -> UUID; func deleteTrip(tripID: UUID) throws; func markTripDidNotHappen(tripID: UUID) throws }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"SwiftData-backed storage boundary for trip lifecycle."},
  {"name":"GeneratedListRepository","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol GeneratedListRepository { func loadItems(tripID: UUID) throws -> [PackingItem]; func replaceSuggestedItems(tripID: UUID, items: [PackingItem]) throws; func toggleChecked(tripID: UUID, itemID: UUID, isChecked: Bool) throws; func upsertCustomItem(tripID: UUID, name: String, quantity: Int, category: PackingCategory) throws; func removeItem(tripID: UUID, itemID: UUID) throws }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Works against canonical PackingItem rows, not a separate persisted artifact type."},
  {"name":"ReviewRepository","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol ReviewRepository { func loadReview(tripID: UUID) throws -> Review?; func saveReview(tripID: UUID, outcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome], wishIPacked: String?) throws }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Save path is full overwrite semantics so review edits remain deterministic."},
  {"name":"PackingCoordinator","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol PackingCoordinator { func createTripAndGenerate(_ draft: TripDraft) async throws -> UUID; func regenerateList(for tripID: UUID) async throws; func submitReview(tripID: UUID, outcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome], wishIPacked: String?) async throws; func rebuildAllDerivedState() async throws }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Only cross-lane bridge between SwiftData models and pure generator. rebuildAllDerivedState is required for edit recovery, migration repair, and QA verification."},
  {"name":"EntitlementSnapshot","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct EntitlementSnapshot: Equatable { let isPremiumActive: Bool; let activeProductID: String?; let lastVerifiedAt: Date?; let graceWindowEndsAt: Date? }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Single entitlement state surface for gating and offline grace behavior."},
  {"name":"EntitlementStoreProtocol","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"@MainActor protocol EntitlementStoreProtocol: AnyObject { var current: EntitlementSnapshot { get } func start() async; func refresh() async }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Long-lived StoreKit observer listening to Transaction.updates, never a one-shot launch check."},
  {"name":"isWithinFreeRetentionWindow","kind":"function","language":"Swift","signature":"func isWithinFreeRetentionWindow(tripId: UUID, tripDate: Date, allTripDatesById: [UUID: Date], isPaidEntitled: Bool) -> Bool","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Pure, cheap, relationship-free free-tier gate. Safe to run in list rendering."},
  {"name":"WidgetSnapshot","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct WidgetSnapshot: Codable, Equatable { let stableTripId: UUID?; let daysUntilTrip: Int?; let percentComplete: Double?; let hasPendingReview: Bool; let updatedAt: Date }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"One shared file with dual target membership. No destination text by construction."},
  {"name":"WidgetSnapshotPublisher","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol WidgetSnapshotPublisher { func publish(_ snapshot: WidgetSnapshot) async }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Debounces app-group writes and WidgetCenter reloads over short inactivity windows."},
  {"name":"PendingNavigationIntent","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum PendingNavigationIntent: Equatable { case openTrip(UUID); case openReview(UUID); case openSettings; case openPaywall; case none }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Single app-root routing surface for notification, widget, and Siri entry points."},
  {"name":"TabRoute","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum TabRoute: Hashable { case tripList, tripDetail(UUID), review(UUID), insights, settings, paywall }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Independent NavigationPath element per tab."},
  {"name":"DeepLinkResolution","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum DeepLinkResolution: Equatable { case route(TabRoute); case tripNotFound }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"tripNotFound reuses existing toast system rather than inventing a second error presentation."},
  {"name":"DeepLinkRouter","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol DeepLinkRouter { func resolve(_ intent: PendingNavigationIntent, context: ModelContext) -> DeepLinkResolution }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Prevents racing entry points from independently mutating navigation state."},
  {"name":"ToastMessage","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct ToastMessage: Equatable { let text: String; let style: ToastStyle }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Shared toast payload for changed-list moment and trip-not-found fallback."},
  {"name":"ToastStyle","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum ToastStyle: Equatable { case info, success, warning }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Minimal shared styles only."},
  {"name":"ToastCenter","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"@Observable final class ToastCenter { func show(_ message: ToastMessage) }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Single implementation only; no duplicate ad hoc banners."},
  {"name":"NotificationScheduler","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol NotificationScheduler { func scheduleReviewNudge(for tripId: UUID, at date: Date) }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Best-effort only. Permission denial never blocks any core flow."},
  {"name":"AddPackingItemIntent","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct AddPackingItemIntent: AppIntent { @Parameter var itemName: String; func perform() async throws -> some IntentResult }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Lives in main app target and calls domain services directly; targets most imminent trip."},
  {"name":"ExportingPackingList","kind":"protocol","language":"Swift","signature":"protocol ExportingPackingList { func exportPlainTextList(for tripID: UUID) throws -> URL }","owning_lane":"services_utilities","notes":"Paid feature surface. Export strips personal analytic reasoning."},
  {"name":"ColorStory","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum ColorStory: CaseIterable { case hotArid, tropicalHumid, temperateMild, coldContinental, alpine, mediterranean }","owning_lane":"primary_ui","notes":"Pure UI enum derived from climate/season rules; maps to locked design tokens."},
  {"name":"AccessibilityCollapseAdapter","kind":"function","language":"Swift","signature":"func collapsedHeaderStyle(colorStory: ColorStory, dynamicTypeSize: DynamicTypeSize, increaseContrast: Bool, reduceTransparency: Bool) -> HeaderRenderStyle","owning_lane":"polish_resilience","notes":"One simplification path reused across XXL Dynamic Type, Increase Contrast, and Reduce Transparency."},
  {"name":"AppError","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum AppError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case climateDataMissing(String); case generationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case tripNotFound; case invalidTripDates; case notificationDenied }","owning_lane":"polish_resilience","notes":"Typed reachable failures for UI error states and verification."},
  {"name":"PersistenceBootstrap","kind":"function","language":"Swift","signature":"func makeModelContainer() throws -> ModelContainer","owning_lane":"polish_resilience","notes":"Builds VersionedSchema-backed container. Failure must surface a full-screen error state, never crash-loop."},
  {"name":"BuildVerificationChecklist","kind":"struct","language":"Swift","signature":"struct BuildVerificationChecklist { let verifiedCoreFlows: [String]; let unverifiableInHeadless: [String]; let knownLimitations: [String] }","owning_lane":"polish_resilience","notes":"Shared vocabulary for final verification so build and review report the same truth."}
]}```

**Claude (Frontend Engineer · User Advocate) — Round 2**

Okay, reading Codex's full round-1 turn now, and the coordinator's right that there's a real fork here, but I think it's narrower than it looks once I read closely — Codex's prose actually describes SwiftData records (`TripRecord`, `GeneratedListRecord`, `PackingItemRecord`, `ReviewRecord`) that are structurally almost identical to my `Trip`/`PackingItem`/`Review`, and then separately his interfaces-json defines value-type `PackingListArtifact`/`PackingItemArtifact` structs with their own `GeneratedListRepository.saveArtifact`/`loadCurrentArtifact`. So the real question isn't "class vs struct," it's whether the frozen generated list gets persisted as *one JSON-blob artifact per generation event* or as *individual `PackingItem` rows with the frozen fields written directly onto them*. I want to push hard against the blob version, because it's a real performance and correctness trap for exactly the interaction this app lives or dies on: checking items off while packing. If `PackingListArtifact` is the thing that's actually saved, then toggling one checkbox means decode-the-whole-artifact, flip one boolean inside a nested `[PackingSectionArtifact]` array, re-encode, re-save — every single tap. That's needless churn for a local-first app that's supposed to feel instant, and it also creates a second source of truth question I don't love: is the "artifact" authoritative, or are the individual checked-states inside it just mutated in place, in which case what does "frozen artifact" even mean anymore once you're mutating fields inside it post-generation?

My proposal to actually resolve this: the SwiftData rows *are* the one and only persisted representation — no separate artifact table. `GenerationEngine.generate(input:) -> GenerationResult` is still exactly the pure value-type contract Codex wants at the boundary (this is literally what the architecture review meant by "written back through ModelContext... as one immutable generated-list artifact" — the artifact is real, it's just that its persisted form *is* the set of `PackingItem` rows with `suggestionReason`/`isRecentlyChanged` frozen onto them, applied in one write via a function like `applyGenerationResult(_:to:)`, not a parallel JSON blob living alongside them). Then for the two places Codex correctly identified that need a clean value-type view — export/share, and the pure engine's own input/output — we derive a lightweight `PackingItemSnapshot` on demand from the `@Model` rows via a simple mapping function, rather than persisting it twice. One source of truth, cheap incremental writes for the interaction that happens twenty times per packing session, and Codex's clean value-type boundary for generation and export both survive intact. I think this is a case where my simpler storage plus his value-type discipline at the boundary is strictly better than either alone.

Second thing I want to flatly reject: `TripStatus { planned, completed, didNotHappen }` as *persisted* state. Think about what "completed" actually means here — it's just "the end date has passed," which is a pure function of `endDate` and the current date, not a fact that needs to be written to disk. If we persist it as an enum, now we need something to flip it from `planned` to `completed` at the right moment — a background task, a check on every launch, something — and if that check doesn't run (app not opened around the transition, or someone edits a trip's dates and the enum doesn't get re-derived), you get a trip sitting in the wrong state indefinitely with nothing forcing a correction. That's a real bug class, and it's completely avoidable: `didHappen: Bool` stays the only thing actually persisted (because it's a real fact someone asserted, not a derivable one), and everything else — upcoming, in-progress, awaiting review, reviewed — comes from a pure `tripPhase(trip:now:) -> TripPhase` function computed fresh every time it's needed, same discipline as the lock-status check. Codex, I think you'd actually agree with this once it's framed as "don't persist what's a pure function of data you already have" — it's the same instinct behind not persisting a cached learning-history rollup.

Third: I don't buy `ItemUsageOutcome.wishedPacked` as a per-item outcome case. "Wish I'd packed X" is, by design (locked back in the design-handoff phase), a single free-text field on the review as a whole — it's for an item that was never on the list at all, suggested or custom, so there's no `stableItemKey` to attach a `ReviewedItemOutcome` to in the first place. Folding it into the same enum as `used`/`unused` implies a UI where you'd tap an existing row and mark it "wishedPacked," which isn't the actual review flow — the actual flow is: exceptions get toggled unused, and separately there's one open text box. I'd keep `ReviewItemOutcome.wasUsed: Bool` for real per-item signal, and `Review.wishIPacked: String?` as its own field entirely disconnected from item identity. Simpler, and it matches the screen that was already speced.

Now, credit where it's due, because Codex had a few ideas in his json that are straight-up better than what I had. Splitting `SuggestionReason` into `kind: SuggestionReasonKind` plus `message: String` instead of my flat `reason: String` is a real improvement — it means tests can pin on the *kind* (did this promote via reviewed history vs. climate default) even in cases where we don't want to hardcode exact wording, and it gives the reason sheet a clean way to group/format consistently. I'm adopting that outright. Same with `algorithmVersion` — stamping each generation with a version number is cheap insurance for exactly the kind of "why did trip A and trip B get different treatment" debugging session we'll have eventually, so I'll hang `generationAlgorithmVersion: Int` directly on `Trip` next to `lastGeneratedAt`. `TripSummary` as the explicit cheap list-projection type is also better than leaving it implicit the way I did — naming it forces the discipline I was gesturing at (no relationship access from the row). And `AppError` plus `BuildVerificationChecklist` are good, uncontested, and I'll keep them verbatim — that second one in particular is exactly the kind of thing that keeps the eventual "what's actually done vs. flagged as a limitation" conversation honest instead of vibes-based. Last small merge: I'll keep raw `ClimateNormal` as the stored/display data (average highs/lows/precip in real units — you want the actual numbers for the climate card copy), but adopt `PrecipitationBand` as a derived, computed-not-stored classification the generation engine uses for its coarse decision rules, via a pure `precipitationBand(for:) -> PrecipitationBand` function. Raw data for humans, band for the engine — best of both.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "TripType", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum TripType: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case business, cityWeekend, beachWarm, coldWinter, outdoorHiking, familyKids }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Closed six-item taxonomy, agreed by both contributions."},
  {"name": "PackingCategory", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PackingCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case clothing, toiletries, electronics, documents, health, gear }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Closed set for checklist section grouping."},
  {"name": "Trip", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class Trip { var id: UUID; var destinationDisplayName: String; var regionId: String?; var climateZoneFallbackId: String?; var startDate: Date; var endDate: Date; var tripType: TripType; var carryOnOnly: Bool; var laundryAccess: Bool; var didHappen: Bool; var createdAt: Date; var lastGeneratedAt: Date?; var generationAlgorithmVersion: Int?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [PackingItem]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var review: Review? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "SOLE persisted representation — no separate PackingListArtifact table. didHappen is the only persisted lifecycle flag; planned/completed/awaiting-review are always computed via tripPhase(), never stored, so nothing can get stuck in a stale state."},
  {"name": "TripPhase", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum TripPhase { case upcoming, inProgressOrAwaitingReturn, awaitingReview, reviewed, didNotHappen }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Never persisted. Purely derived so it can't drift from reality."},
  {"name": "tripPhase", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func tripPhase(trip: Trip, now: Date) -> TripPhase", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Computed from endDate + review presence + didHappen. Replaces Codex's persisted TripStatus enum by design."},
  {"name": "PackingItem", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class PackingItem { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var name: String; var category: PackingCategory; var isCustom: Bool; var isChecked: Bool; var quantity: Int; var reasonKind: SuggestionReasonKind?; var reasonMessage: String?; var reasonEvidenceCount: Int?; var isRecentlyChanged: Bool; var sortOrder: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "reasonKind/reasonMessage/isRecentlyChanged frozen at generation/regeneration time only, applied in one write via applyGenerationResult. isCustom items are never touched by regeneration diffing."},
  {"name": "SuggestionReasonKind", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum SuggestionReasonKind: String, Codable { case tripTypeDefault, climateDefault, repeatedManualAdd, reviewedHistoryPromoted, reviewedHistorySuppressed, quantityAdjusted }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Adopted from Codex's round-1 proposal — lets tests pin on kind independent of exact wording, and gives the reason sheet a formatting taxonomy."},
  {"name": "SuggestionReason", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct SuggestionReason: Codable, Equatable { let kind: SuggestionReasonKind; let message: String; let evidenceCount: Int? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Value-type shape produced by GenerationEngine; mapped onto PackingItem's reasonKind/reasonMessage/reasonEvidenceCount fields at write time."},
  {"name": "Review", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class Review { var id: UUID; var trip: Trip?; var completedAt: Date; var wishIPacked: String?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var itemOutcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "wishIPacked is a single free-text field disconnected from any item id — it covers items never on the list at all, so it cannot be folded into a per-item outcome enum."},
  {"name": "ReviewItemOutcome", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class ReviewItemOutcome { var id: UUID; var review: Review?; var itemName: String; var category: PackingCategory; var wasUsed: Bool }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Only source of learning signal; checklist-checking during packing never writes here. Deliberately a plain Bool, not a 3-case enum — 'wished I'd packed' lives on Review.wishIPacked instead."},
  {"name": "ClimateNormal", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ClimateNormal: Codable { let regionOrZoneId: String; let month: Int; let averageHighC: Double; let averageLowC: Double; let averagePrecipitationMM: Double; let notes: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Raw bundled data used for climate-card display copy. Always 'historical average', never forecast language."},
  {"name": "PrecipitationBand", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PrecipitationBand: String, Codable { case dry, moderate, wet }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Derived, not stored. Adopted from Codex — coarse classification the generation engine uses for decision rules, computed from raw ClimateNormal."},
  {"name": "precipitationBand", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func precipitationBand(for normal: ClimateNormal) -> PrecipitationBand", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure derivation, called by GenerationEngine, not persisted anywhere."},
  {"name": "ClimateCatalog", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ClimateCatalog { func normal(for regionOrZoneId: String, month: Int) -> ClimateNormal?; func allRegions() -> [RegionCatalogEntry]; func allFallbackZones() -> [ClimateZoneFallbackEntry] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Loaded once in-memory at launch; CI test asserts every region/zone has all 12 months present."},
  {"name": "RegionCatalogEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RegionCatalogEntry: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let displayName: String; let climateZoneFallbackId: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "~30-40 bundled destinations."},
  {"name": "ClimateZoneFallbackEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ClimateZoneFallbackEntry: Codable, Identifiable { let id: String; let displayName: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Manual fallback bucket shown inline the instant destination search returns zero matches."},
  {"name": "monthOfMajorityDays", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func monthOfMajorityDays(start: Date, end: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> Int", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Deterministic month-boundary resolution for climate lookup."},
  {"name": "LearningHistorySnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct LearningHistorySnapshot { struct ItemEvidence { let itemName: String; let category: PackingCategory; let usedCount: Int; let unusedCount: Int; let lastSeenDate: Date }; let evidenceByItem: [ItemEvidence]; let minimumEvidenceCount: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "minimumEvidenceCount = 2 same-type-or-region data points before a suggestion visibly shifts, per detailed_discussion."},
  {"name": "LearningHistoryReader", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol LearningHistoryReader { func snapshot(for tripType: TripType, regionOrZoneId: String, context: ModelContext) -> LearningHistorySnapshot }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Always recomputes fresh from raw ReviewItemOutcome rows on trips where didHappen == true; never an incremental/cached rollup, so review edits recompute deterministically."},
  {"name": "GenerationInput", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GenerationInput { let tripType: TripType; let month: Int; let regionOrZoneId: String; let tripLengthDays: Int; let carryOnOnly: Bool; let laundryAccess: Bool; let climate: [ClimateNormal]; let learningHistory: LearningHistorySnapshot }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fully snapshotted value type; nothing here is a live SwiftData reference."},
  {"name": "GenerationResult", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GenerationResult { struct SuggestedItem { let name: String; let category: PackingCategory; let quantity: Int; let reason: SuggestionReason }; let items: [SuggestedItem]; let algorithmVersion: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure output value type. Never persisted as its own artifact table — applyGenerationResult maps it directly onto PackingItem rows in one write."},
  {"name": "GenerationEngine", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol GenerationEngine { func generate(input: GenerationInput) -> GenerationResult }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "MUST stay synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated — the rule that keeps trip creation feeling instant, not 'generating'."},
  {"name": "applyGenerationResult", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func applyGenerationResult(_ result: GenerationResult, to trip: Trip, context: ModelContext) -> [PackingItem]", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Matches new suggested items against existing suggested PackingItem rows by name; unchanged reason keeps checked state and isRecentlyChanged=false, otherwise isRecentlyChanged=true. Never touches isCustom rows. This IS the 'frozen artifact write', done as row mutation not blob replacement."},
  {"name": "PackingItemSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PackingItemSnapshot: Codable, Equatable { let name: String; let category: PackingCategory; let quantity: Int; let isCustom: Bool; let reason: SuggestionReason? }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Lightweight value-type view derived on demand from PackingItem rows for export/share; never separately persisted. Export strips reason entirely per standing privacy rule."},
  {"name": "isWithinFreeRetentionWindow", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func isWithinFreeRetentionWindow(tripId: UUID, tripDate: Date, allTripDatesById: [UUID: Date], isPaidEntitled: Bool) -> Bool", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Relationship-free; takes only id+date projections plus entitlement state."},
  {"name": "TripSummary", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct TripSummary: Identifiable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let destinationDisplayName: String; let dateRangeText: String; let tripType: TripType; let progress: Double; let isLockedByFreeTier: Bool; let phase: TripPhase }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Adopted from Codex — cheap list-facing projection; TripCard must only ever read this shape, never trip.items/trip.review."},
  {"name": "TabRoute", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum TabRoute { case tripList, tripDetail(UUID), review(UUID), insights, settings, paywall(PaywallContext) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "NavigationPath element; each of the three tabs owns its own independent path array, reset to root on cold relaunch."},
  {"name": "PendingNavigationIntent", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PendingNavigationIntent { case openTrip(UUID); case openReview(UUID); case openPaywall; case none }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Single app-root value resolved once per external trigger via DeepLinkRouter."},
  {"name": "DeepLinkRouter", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol DeepLinkRouter { func resolve(_ intent: PendingNavigationIntent, context: ModelContext) -> DeepLinkResolution }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "tripNotFound drives the same ToastCenter used for the changed-list moment, different copy only."},
  {"name": "DeepLinkResolution", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum DeepLinkResolution { case route(TabRoute); case tripNotFound }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Never crashes/no-ops on a stale deep link pointing at a deleted or didNotHappen trip."},
  {"name": "ToastCenter", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable final class ToastCenter { func show(_ message: ToastMessage) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "One shared component for changed-list moment and tripNotFound fallback."},
  {"name": "ToastMessage", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ToastMessage { let text: String; let style: ToastStyle }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Two copy variants, one component."},
  {"name": "ColorStory", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ColorStory: CaseIterable { case hotArid, tropicalHumid, temperateMild, coldContinental, alpine, mediterranean; func hexPair() -> (primary: String, secondary: String); func textColor(onLuminance: Double) -> Color }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Pure function of climate zone + season; formula-driven contrast per design_discussion's locked hex values."},
  {"name": "EntitlementState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct EntitlementState: Equatable { let isPremiumActive: Bool; let lastVerifiedAt: Date?; let graceWindowEndsAt: Date? }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Merged naming from both round-1 proposals."},
  {"name": "EntitlementStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol EntitlementStore: AnyObject { var current: EntitlementState { get }; func start() async; func refresh() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Long-lived Transaction.updates listener, never a one-shot launch check. Offline grace window biases toward preserving unlocked access."},
  {"name": "WidgetSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct WidgetSnapshot: Codable, Equatable { let stableTripId: UUID?; let daysUntilTrip: Int?; let percentComplete: Double?; let hasPendingReview: Bool; let updatedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "One file, dual target membership (main app + widget extension). Never contains destination text by construction."},
  {"name": "WidgetSnapshotPublisher", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol WidgetSnapshotPublisher { func publish(_ snapshot: WidgetSnapshot) async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Debounced ~1-2s over inactivity, then calls WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines."},
  {"name": "AddPackingItemIntent", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AddPackingItemIntent: AppIntent { @Parameter var itemName: String; func perform() async throws -> some IntentResult }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Lives in main app target, not widget extension; calls domain services directly, targets most imminent upcoming trip."},
  {"name": "NotificationScheduler", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol NotificationScheduler { func scheduleReviewNudge(for tripId: UUID, at date: Date) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Best-effort only; permission denial never blocks core flow. Generic copy, no destination text."},
  {"name": "ExportingPackingList", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ExportingPackingList { func exportPlainTextList(for tripID: UUID) throws -> URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Builds PackingItemSnapshot values on demand and strips all reasoning before writing the export file."},
  {"name": "AppError", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AppError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case climateDataMissing(String); case generationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case tripNotFound; case invalidTripDates; case notificationDenied }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Adopted from Codex verbatim; shared typed errors across UI and services."},
  {"name": "AccessibilityCollapseAdapter", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func collapsedHeaderStyle(colorStory: ColorStory, dynamicTypeSize: DynamicTypeSize, increaseContrast: Bool, reduceTransparency: Bool) -> HeaderRenderStyle", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "One shared 'collapse to flat/simple' mechanism reused across Dynamic Type XXL, Increase Contrast, and Reduce Transparency."},
  {"name": "PersistenceBootstrap", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func makeModelContainer() throws -> ModelContainer", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "VersionedSchema (SchemaV1) from first commit; failure surfaces a full-bleed persistence-error state at app root, never a crash."},
  {"name": "BuildVerificationChecklist", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct BuildVerificationChecklist { let verifiedCoreFlows: [String]; let unverifiableInHeadless: [String]; let knownLimitations: [String] }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Adopted from Codex verbatim; shared vocabulary for build verification and final review."}
]}
```

**Gemini — Round 2 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Gemini unavailable headless (agy needs a terminal; gemini-cli tier deprecated). gemini(key) -> Warning: 256-color support not detected. Using a terminal with at least 256-color support is recomme | gemini CLI -> auth/tier error_

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 2**

Round 2 closed almost everything. Both Codex and Claude threw out the idea of a separate persisted "artifact blob" — they landed in the same place from different directions: Codex explicitly reframed his own round-1 proposal ("the canonical stored model is SwiftData rows... the generator never sees those classes directly, it snapshots into `GenerationInput`/`GenerationResult`, and a coordinator applies that result back onto `PackingItem` rows in one commit"), and Claude made the sharper case for why the blob version would actually break the app's core interaction (every checkbox tap during packing would mean decode-mutate-reencode a whole nested artifact, for no benefit). So that fork is resolved: `Trip`/`PackingItem`/`Review`/`ReviewItemOutcome` are the one and only persisted shape, the generation engine stays pure value-types-in/value-types-out, and a single `applyGenerationResult` function is the one place that writes frozen reason/changed-flag facts onto rows.

They also independently converged on the review-outcome shape without needing to argue about it — both dropped the richer `ItemUsageOutcome` enum in favor of a plain `wasUsed: Bool` on `ReviewItemOutcome` plus a separate `wishIPacked: String?` on `Review` itself, since "wish I'd packed X" has no `stableItemKey` to attach to. And Claude adopted several of Codex's round-1 ideas outright as improvements: splitting reason into `kind` + `message` (so tests can pin on category without hardcoding exact copy), stamping a generation algorithm version, naming `TripSummary` as an explicit cheap projection type, and keeping `AppError`/`BuildVerificationChecklist` verbatim. Nobody's contesting any of that.

The one thing still genuinely open: Codex's `Trip` model persists a `TripStatus` enum (`planned`/`completed`/`didNotHappen`) as stored state. Claude argues against persisting "completed" specifically, since it's just "end date has passed" — a pure function of data already on hand — and persisting it invites a stale-state bug if nothing ever runs the check that flips it (an edited trip, an app that isn't opened around the transition date, etc.). Claude's counter-proposal keeps only `didHappen: Bool` as real persisted fact and computes everything else (upcoming/in-progress/awaiting-review/reviewed) fresh via a pure `tripPhase(trip:now:)` function, same discipline as the free-tier lock check. This lands in Claude's round-2 turn, which came after Codex's, so Codex hasn't had a chance to respond to that specific argument yet.

CONSENSUS: NO

One thing left for round 3: Codex needs to weigh in on persisted `TripStatus` vs. `didHappen: Bool` + computed `TripPhase`. Given Codex's own stated principle elsewhere in this phase ("don't persist what's a pure function of data you already have" — the same reasoning he'd apply to not caching a learning-history rollup), this looks likely to resolve toward Claude's version, but it needs to actually be said rather than assumed.

  {"name": "NotificationScheduler", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol NotificationScheduler { func scheduleReviewNudge(for tripId: UUID, at date: Date) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Agreed by both; best-effort only, generic copy, permission denial never blocks core flow."},
  {"name": "ExportingPackingList", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol ExportingPackingList { func exportPlainTextList(for tripID: UUID) throws -> URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Agreed by both; builds PackingItemSnapshot values on demand, strips all reasoning."},
  {"name": "TripRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol TripRepository { func fetchSummaries() throws -> [TripSummary]; func fetchTrip(id: UUID) throws -> Trip; func upsert(_ draft: TripDraft) throws -> UUID; func deleteTrip(tripID: UUID) throws; func markTripDidNotHappen(tripID: UUID) throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "From Codex's round 2, uncontested; SwiftData-backed storage boundary for trip lifecycle."},
  {"name": "GeneratedListRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol GeneratedListRepository { func loadItems(tripID: UUID) throws -> [PackingItem]; func replaceSuggestedItems(tripID: UUID, items: [PackingItem]) throws; func toggleChecked(tripID: UUID, itemID: UUID, isChecked: Bool) throws; func upsertCustomItem(tripID: UUID, name: String, quantity: Int, category: PackingCategory) throws; func removeItem(tripID: UUID, itemID: UUID) throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "From Codex's round 2, uncontested; works against canonical PackingItem rows directly, consistent with the resolved no-blob decision."},
  {"name": "ReviewRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol ReviewRepository { func loadReview(tripID: UUID) throws -> Review?; func saveReview(tripID: UUID, outcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome], wishIPacked: String?) throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "From Codex's round 2, uncontested; full-overwrite save semantics so review edits stay deterministic."},
  {"name": "PackingCoordinator", "kind": "protocol", "language": "Swift", "signature": "protocol PackingCoordinator { func createTripAndGenerate(_ draft: TripDraft) async throws -> UUID; func regenerateList(for tripID: UUID) async throws; func submitReview(tripID: UUID, outcomes: [ReviewItemOutcome], wishIPacked: String?) async throws; func rebuildAllDerivedState() async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "From Codex's round 2, uncontested; only cross-lane bridge between SwiftData models and the pure generator, and the required rebuild-all-derived-state path for edit recovery/migration repair/QA verification."},
  {"name": "AppError", "kind": "enum", "language": "Swift", "signature": "enum AppError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case climateDataMissing(String); case generationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case tripNotFound; case invalidTripDates; case notificationDenied }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Agreed by both, adopted verbatim."},
  {"name": "AccessibilityCollapseAdapter", "kind": "function", "language": "Swift", "signature": "func collapsedHeaderStyle(colorStory: ColorStory, dynamicTypeSize: DynamicTypeSize, increaseContrast: Bool, reduceTransparency: Bool) -> HeaderRenderStyle", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Agreed by both; one shared collapse mechanism for XXL Dynamic Type, Increase Contrast, Reduce Transparency."},
  {"name": "PersistenceBootstrap", "kind": "function", "language": "Swift", "signature": "func makeModelContainer() throws -> ModelContainer", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Agreed by both; VersionedSchema (SchemaV1) from first commit, failure surfaces full-bleed error state, never crash."},
  {"name": "BuildVerificationChecklist", "kind": "struct", "language": "Swift", "signature": "struct BuildVerificationChecklist { let verifiedCoreFlows: [String]; let unverifiableInHeadless: [String]; let knownLimitations: [String] }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Agreed by both, adopted verbatim."}
]}
```

### Round 3


**Codex (QA & Risk · Pragmatist) — Round 3**

Claude’s right to push on the `TripStatus` thing, and I don’t think we should leave any ambiguity here because it’s exactly the kind of tiny “harmless” persisted field that becomes a stale-state bug six weeks later. I would not persist `planned` or `completed`. Persist only the facts the user or system actually asserts and that cannot be derived cheaply: dates, review presence, and whether the trip did not happen. Everything else should be computed. If a trip ended yesterday and nobody opened the app at midnight to flip some stored status bit, the UI still needs to be right. Same if the user edits the end date after generation, or restores onto a new device later. So I’m firmly on Claude’s side here: keep `didHappen: Bool?` or an equivalent “did not happen” fact as real stored data, and compute `TripPhase` every time from `now`, `endDate`, `review != nil`, and that did-happen flag. That matches the rest of the architecture we already agreed on: don’t persist what’s a pure function of data you already have, especially when stale derivations would leak straight into the UI and tests.

I’d make one small sharpening pass, though, because the current `Bool` naming is a little slippery for QA. A plain `didHappen: Bool` invites the “what is the default before the trip occurs?” bug. If it defaults `false`, a brand-new planned trip can accidentally look like “didn’t happen.” If it defaults `true`, now the flag isn’t really expressing user intent either. The safer MVP shape is either `didHappen: Bool?` with `nil` meaning “not yet resolved,” or a tiny persisted enum that only captures the non-derivable user decision, like `TripOccurrenceState { case assumedHappened, didNotHappen }`, defaulting to the first. But if we want to stay minimal and not reopen naming bikeshedding, I can live with the current field as long as the notes make the semantics explicit: it is only flipped by the explicit “this trip didn’t happen” flow, never by the clock, and `TripPhase` is always computed. That’s the smallest thing that ships cleanly and is testable. With that, I think the contract is actually coherent enough for parallel build lanes without anybody inventing incompatible assumptions.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces":[
  {"name":"TripType","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum TripType: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case business, cityWeekend, beachWarm, coldWinter, outdoorHiking, familyKids }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Closed six-item taxonomy."},
  {"name":"PackingCategory","kind":"enum","language":"Swift","signature":"enum PackingCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case clothing, toiletries, electronics, documents, health, gear }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Closed checklist grouping set."},

No persisted planned/completed status. didHappen is only changed by the explicit 'didn't happen' flow; all display state is computed via tripPhase."},
