```tasks-json
{"id":"T-006","title":"Pure derivation functions: tripPhase, monthOfMajorityDays, isWithinFreeRetentionWindow","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/TripPhase.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/monthOfMajorityDays.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/isWithinFreeRetentionWindow.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003","T-005"],"acceptance_criteria":["month-boundary tie-break rule is deterministic and unit-tested with a trip that spans exactly two months","isWithinFreeRetentionWindow is relationship-free (takes only ids/dates/counts, never a SwiftData relationship traversal) and unit-tested at the exact 3rd/4th trip boundary","tripPhase is never persisted and is recomputed correctly for every TripPhase case including didNotHappen"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-007","title":"Pure GenerationEngine and the learning go/no-go test","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationInput.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationResult.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/SuggestionReason.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationEngine.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Learning/LearningHistorySnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Tests/DataDomainTests/GenerationEngineGoNoGoTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-005","T-006"],"acceptance_criteria":["generate(input:) is synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated","reviewing trip A with an item marked unused, then generating a same-type-or-region trip B, produces that item visibly demoted with a reason string naming trip A","a single data point never shifts a suggestion; the minimumEvidenceCount=2 rule is explicitly tested","zero-history input still produces a non-empty, sensible list from trip type + climate alone"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-008","title":"applyGenerationResult and SwiftData-backed LearningHistoryReader","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/applyGenerationResult.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Learning/LearningHistoryReader.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003","T-007"],"acceptance_criteria":["applying a regenerated result preserves isCustom rows and preserves checked state on unchanged suggested rows","isRecentlyChanged is set only on rows whose reason actually changed from the prior frozen snapshot","LearningHistoryReader recomputes fresh from raw ReviewItemOutcome rows every call, never an incremental cache, and only reads trips where didHappen == true"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-009","title":"Trip/GeneratedList/Review repositories","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Repositories/TripRepository.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Repositories/GeneratedListRepository.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Repositories/ReviewRepository.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003"],"acceptance_criteria":["checked state, custom items, and a saved review all survive a real kill-and-relaunch in an integration test","deleting a trip cascade-deletes items and review; markTripDidNotHappen only flips didHappen and never deletes","toggleChecked performs a single targeted field write, verified by asserting only one row changes, not a full-list re-save","saveReview is full-overwrite so re-editing a review is deterministic, never incremental patching"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-010","title":"PackingCoordinator: the only bridge between SwiftData and the pure engine","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Coordinator/PackingCoordinator.swift"],"depends_on":["T-007","T-008","T-009"],"acceptance_criteria":["createTripAndGenerate, regenerateList, submitReview, and rebuildAllDerivedState are all integration-tested against real repositories","generation always runs synchronously on the main actor with no real suspension point; a test asserts no spinner is needed on the golden path","rebuildAllDerivedState correctly repairs a deliberately corrupted derived-state fixture in a test"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-011","title":"Trip creation and edit flow","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/TripCreateEditView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/TripCreateEditStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/DestinationSearchRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/TripTypeChip.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/RegenerationBanner.swift"],"depends_on":["T-002","T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["a brand-new user with zero history creates a trip fully offline and gets a non-empty, reason-stringed list in the same session","destination search shows the climate-zone fallback the instant results are empty, with no debounce/async/loading state","editing a trip's dates/destination regenerates suggested items but preserves custom items and checked state, showing the RegenerationBanner only when the diff actually affects generation"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-012","title":"Trip list and trip detail/checklist screens","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/TripListView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/TripDetailView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/TripListStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/TripDetailStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/TripCard.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ColorStoryHeaderView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ProgressRingView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ChecklistRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/CustomItemRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ReasonSheet.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Design/ColorStory.swift"],"depends_on":["T-011"],"acceptance_criteria":["empty/loading/success/error states all present on both screens, plus a distinct 'generating' state on trip detail separate from ordinary loading","checked state and custom items survive a real kill-and-relaunch","every suggested item's reason sheet shows a real, specific reason string, never generic 'smart suggestion' copy","locked (beyond-free-tier) trip cards render instantly using isWithinFreeRetentionWindow with no fetch into items/review"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-013","title":"Post-trip review flow including didn't-happen exit","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/PostTripReviewView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/ReviewStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ReviewExceptionRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ConfirmationDialog.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010","T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["a zero-exception review completes in 3 taps or fewer, measured by an XCUITest, not eyeballed","the 'this trip didn't happen' exit is visually and semantically distinct from a normal review and requires its own confirmation","a completed review is reopenable and editable, and downstream aggregates recompute deterministically from full history, not incrementally"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-014","title":"Changed-item badge and first-time toast for the review-driven regeneration moment","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ChangedItemBadge.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ToastCenter.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ToastMessage.swift"],"depends_on":["T-013"],"acceptance_criteria":["after a review-driven regeneration, affected items show a persistent inline badge","exactly one banner/toast ever fires for this moment across the app's lifetime, gated by a single permanent flag that never resets","the badge clears when its item is checked off"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-015","title":"EntitlementStore and StoreKit 2 wiring","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Entitlements/EntitlementState.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Entitlements/EntitlementStore.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001","T-009"],"acceptance_criteria":["a long-lived Transaction.updates listener drives entitlement state, never just a launch-time check","a simulated purchase and a simulated expiration/renewal-failure are both exercised against the local .storekit config","an offline grace window biases toward preserving already-unlocked access rather than flapping locked/unlocked","trip creation and the core packing flow never block on entitlement checks"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-016","title":"Paywall and Settings screens","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/PaywallView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/SettingsView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/PaywallStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/PaywallSheet.swift"],"depends_on":["T-015","T-018"],"acceptance_criteria":["paywall reflects real transaction state and includes a working restore-purchases action","settings states on-device privacy as a stated feature, shows climate data provenance copy, and gates export/share behind the real entitlement","every destructive action (delete trip, clear history) requires confirmation before proceeding"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-017","title":"Insights screen","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/InsightsView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/InsightsStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/InsightCard.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/EmptyStateView.swift"],"depends_on":["T-013","T-015"],"acceptance_criteria":["an honest empty state shows before any review exists, with no fabricated sample data","populated state shows only real, attributable behavior patterns from review history","beyond the free-tier window, a blurred-but-visible teaser is shown rather than an empty lock screen"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-018","title":"Export/share with reasoning stripped","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Export/PackingItemSnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Export/ExportingPackingList.swift"],"depends_on":["T-009"],"acceptance_criteria":["exported plain-text list contains item names/quantities only, with all reason/analytic commentary stripped, verified by a test asserting the reason strings never appear in export output"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-019","title":"AppError and PersistenceBootstrap full-bleed error handling","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Errors/AppError.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Persistence/PersistenceBootstrap.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003"],"acceptance_criteria":["a simulated ModelContainer init failure renders a full-bleed error state in a test/preview, never a crash","every AppError case maps to a reachable, distinct UI error state somewhere in the app"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-020","title":"Accessibility header collapse adapter","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/HeaderLayoutVariant.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/HeaderRenderStyle.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/AccessibilityCollapseAdapter.swift"],"depends_on":["T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["all four legitimate combinations (full+gradient, full+flat, collapsed+gradient, collapsed+flat) are unit-tested since Dynamic-Type-XXL layout collapse and Increase-Contrast/Reduce-Transparency color flattening are independent and can both be active at once","ColorStoryHeaderView visibly degrades correctly under Dynamic Type XXL and under Increase Contrast/Reduce Transparency"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-021","title":"WidgetSnapshot, publisher, and KitbagWidget extension","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/WidgetSnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/WidgetSnapshotPublisher.swift","KitbagWidget/KitbagWidgetBundle.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingProgressTimelineProvider.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingProgressWidgetView.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001","T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["WidgetSnapshot structurally never contains destination text, verified by a test inspecting its Codable field set","publish is debounced over roughly 1-2 seconds of inactivity before calling WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines","timeline provider output is unit-tested; on-device rendering is explicitly flagged unverifiable in this headless environment via BuildVerificationChecklist, never claimed as working"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-022","title":"Live Activity for an active packing session","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/PackingSessionActivityAttributes.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingSessionLiveActivity.swift"],"depends_on":["T-021"],"acceptance_criteria":["start/update/end logic is unit-tested against the ActivityKit attributes/content-state shape","on-device rendering is flagged unverifiable in this headless environment, not asserted as working","this is the first feature cut if schedule is squeezed, per the standing cut order"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-023","title":"AddPackingItemIntent and NotificationScheduler","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Intents/AddPackingItemIntent.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Notifications/NotificationScheduler.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["the intent targets the most imminent upcoming trip and has a clean spoken failure path at zero trips, unit-tested","a test proves the core packing flow works identically whether notification permission is granted or denied","notification copy is generic and never contains destination text"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-024","title":"DeepLinkRouter and resolution logic","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Navigation/DeepLinkRouter.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Navigation/DeepLinkResolution.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["resolve() returns .tripNotFound for a deleted or didn't-happen trip id instead of crashing or silently no-opping","a single resolution point prevents simultaneous notification/widget/Siri triggers from racing each other"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-025","title":"Wire deep-link intents and toast fallback into the app root","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Navigation/PendingNavigationIntent.swift"],"depends_on":["T-012","T-021","T-023","T-024"],"acceptance_criteria":["tapping a stale notification/widget/Siri deep link lands safely on Trip List with the shared toast reading that the trip is no longer available","AppRootView.swift (owned by T-002) is only modified here to add intent resolution, not restructured"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-026","title":"Accessibility and empty/loading/error-state audit","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Tests/PrimaryUITests/AccessibilityAuditTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-011","T-012","T-013","T-016","T-017"],"acceptance_criteria":["VoiceOver reads item name, checked state, suggested-vs-custom, and reason/changed status in a sensible order on every screen that has them","no screen distinguishes suggested/custom/locked/review-exception by color alone","delete-trip and mark-didn't-happen confirmations have distinctly different VoiceOver labels and copy weight"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-027","title":"Checklist interaction performance verification","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Tests/ServicesUtilitiesTests/ChecklistPerformanceTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-009","T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["toggling a single item in a 100+ item checklist is measured to trigger exactly one targeted write, not a full-list re-fetch or re-save"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-028","title":"Final BuildVerificationChecklist sweep","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Verification/BuildVerificationChecklist.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010","T-014","T-020","T-022","T-025","T-026","T-027"],"acceptance_criteria":["verifiedCoreFlows lists every core workflow that actually has a passing automated test, by name","unverifiableInHeadless lists widget/Live Activity/Siri on-device rendering and live StoreKit purchases explicitly, never marked as working","knownLimitations is populated from whatever this sweep actually finds, not left empty by default"],"status":"pending"}
]}
```

**Gemini — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Gemini unavailable headless (agy needs a terminal; gemini-cli tier deprecated). gemini(key) -> Warning: 256-color support not detected. Using a terminal with at least 256-color support is recomme | gemini CLI -> auth/tier error_

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

Both engineers converged on the same shape for this phase, but at different zoom levels — Codex sketched a coarse 9-task backlog split roughly by lane and milestone, and Claude took that same structure and drove it all the way down into 28 concrete, immediately-startable tasks with exact file paths, explicit forbidden-edit zones, and a real conflict-prevention mechanism. Nothing in Claude's version contradicts Codex's — it's the same dependency chain from the locked project_plan (scaffolding → data domain → generation engine → coordinator → UI → review-to-learning → monetization/insights → native surfaces → hardening), just made buildable.

The load-bearing decision everyone lands on: `project.pbxproj`, entitlements, scheme, and target definitions are a forbidden edit zone for every lane except the one scaffolding task (T-001) that creates them — after that, lanes only add files under their own folder and Xcode's synchronized folder groups pick them up automatically, so there's never a second hand-edit to the project file. Git is set up with one branch per lane cut from `main` right after scaffolding, with rebase-not-merge integration at defined checkpoints (engine go/no-go, zero-history loop, review-to-learning, final) rather than continuous merging. Ownership splits by volatility exactly as decided in project_plan: data_domain (models, climate data, pure generation engine — zero SwiftUI/StoreKit), services_utilities (repositories, coordinator, entitlements, widgets, intents — the only real seam to the outside world, including the one dual-target `WidgetSnapshot.swift` file), primary_ui (every screen/component, never touches `ModelContext`/StoreKit directly), and polish_resilience (scaffolding itself, error handling, accessibility, and final verification sweep). Nobody starts real UI work until the generation engine's go/no-go test (review demotes an item with a named reason, respecting the 2-data-point evidence rule) actually passes — that's the one gate everything else waits on.

No open disagreement — Codex's list is a strict subset of what Claude's expands into, so I'm carrying Claude's 28-task backlog forward as the final one since it's what lets build workers start immediately, per this phase's acceptance bar.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Lane responsibilities:**
- **data_domain**: SwiftData models + VersionedSchema, bundled climate JSON + ClimateCatalog, pure derivation functions (tripPhase, monthOfMajorityDays, isWithinFreeRetentionWindow), the pure GenerationEngine, LearningHistoryReader, applyGenerationResult. Zero SwiftUI/StoreKit imports anywhere in this lane. Fully unit-testable without a simulator.
- **services_utilities**: Repositories (Trip/GeneratedList/Review), PackingCoordinator (the only bridge between SwiftData and the pure engine), EntitlementStore/StoreKit wiring, WidgetSnapshot + publisher + widget extension + Live Activity, NotificationScheduler, AddPackingItemIntent, DeepLinkRouter, export/share. Owns the one dual-target-membership file (WidgetSnapshot.swift).
- **primary_ui**: Every screen, store, and reusable component — navigation shell, trip create/edit, trip list/detail/checklist, post-trip review, changed-item badge/toast, paywall, settings, insights. Never touches ModelContext or StoreKit directly, only the protocols services_utilities exposes.
- **polish_resilience**: Owns the initial project scaffolding (targets, App Group, StoreKit config, git init, folder groups), AppError, PersistenceBootstrap, the accessibility collapse adapter/HeaderRenderStyle, and the final BuildVerificationChecklist sweep plus cross-cutting accessibility/performance audits.

**Handoffs and forbidden edit zones:**
- `Kitbag.xcodeproj/*`, `.entitlements` files, scheme, and target definitions are forbidden to edit after T-001 lands — new files go under each lane's own synchronized folder, no further pbxproj edits, ever. If a lane needs a new target/top-level folder, that's a stop-and-ask.
- No lane starts real implementation against the coordinator until T-007's generation engine go/no-go test passes.
- `AppRootView.swift` (owned by primary_ui's T-002) may only be extended by T-025 to add deep-link intent resolution, never restructured.
- Integration happens via rebase onto `main` at defined checkpoints (post go/no-go, post zero-history loop, post review-to-learning, final), never continuous merge of half-finished interfaces.

**tasks-json backlog:**

```tasks-json
{"id":"T-012","title":"Trip list and trip detail/checklist screens","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/TripListView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/TripDetailView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/TripListStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/TripDetailStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/TripCard.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ColorStoryHeaderView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ProgressRingView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ChecklistRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/CustomItemRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ReasonSheet.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Design/ColorStory.swift"],"depends_on":["T-011"],"acceptance_criteria":["empty/loading/success/error states all present on both screens, plus a distinct 'generating' state on trip detail separate from ordinary loading","checked state and custom items survive a real kill-and-relaunch","every suggested item's reason sheet shows a real, specific reason string, never generic 'smart suggestion' copy","locked (beyond-free-tier) trip cards render instantly using isWithinFreeRetentionWindow with no fetch into items/review"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-013","title":"Post-trip review flow including didn't-happen exit","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/PostTripReviewView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/ReviewStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ReviewExceptionRow.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ConfirmationDialog.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010","T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["a zero-exception review completes in 3 taps or fewer, measured by an XCUITest, not eyeballed","the 'this trip didn't happen' exit is visually and semantically distinct from a normal review and requires its own confirmation","a completed review is reopenable and editable, and downstream aggregates recompute deterministically from full history, not incrementally"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-014","title":"Changed-item badge and first-time toast for the review-driven regeneration moment","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ChangedItemBadge.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ToastCenter.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/ToastMessage.swift"],"depends_on":["T-013"],"acceptance_criteria":["after a review-driven regeneration, affected items show a persistent inline badge","exactly one banner/toast ever fires for this moment across the app's lifetime, gated by a single permanent flag that never resets","the badge clears when its item is checked off"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-015","title":"EntitlementStore and StoreKit 2 wiring","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Entitlements/EntitlementState.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Entitlements/EntitlementStore.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001","T-009"],"acceptance_criteria":["a long-lived Transaction.updates listener drives entitlement state, never just a launch-time check","a simulated purchase and a simulated expiration/renewal-failure are both exercised against the local .storekit config","an offline grace window biases toward preserving already-unlocked access rather than flapping locked/unlocked","trip creation and the core packing flow never block on entitlement checks"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-016","title":"Paywall and Settings screens","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/PaywallView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/SettingsView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/PaywallStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/PaywallSheet.swift"],"depends_on":["T-015","T-018"],"acceptance_criteria":["paywall reflects real transaction state and includes a working restore-purchases action","settings states on-device privacy as a stated feature, shows climate data provenance copy, and gates export/share behind the real entitlement","every destructive action (delete trip, clear history) requires confirmation before proceeding"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-017","title":"Insights screen","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Screens/InsightsView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Stores/InsightsStore.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/InsightCard.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Components/EmptyStateView.swift"],"depends_on":["T-013","T-015"],"acceptance_criteria":["an honest empty state shows before any review exists, with no fabricated sample data","populated state shows only real, attributable behavior patterns from review history","beyond the free-tier window, a blurred-but-visible teaser is shown rather than an empty lock screen"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-018","title":"Export/share with reasoning stripped","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Export/PackingItemSnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Export/ExportingPackingList.swift"],"depends_on":["T-009"],"acceptance_criteria":["exported plain-text list contains item names/quantities only, with all reason/analytic commentary stripped, verified by a test asserting the reason strings never appear in export output"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-019","title":"AppError and PersistenceBootstrap full-bleed error handling","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Errors/AppError.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Persistence/PersistenceBootstrap.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003"],"acceptance_criteria":["a simulated ModelContainer init failure renders a full-bleed error state in a test/preview, never a crash","every AppError case maps to a reachable, distinct UI error state somewhere in the app"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-020","title":"Accessibility header collapse adapter","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/HeaderLayoutVariant.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/HeaderRenderStyle.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Accessibility/AccessibilityCollapseAdapter.swift"],"depends_on":["T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["all four legitimate combinations (full+gradient, full+flat, collapsed+gradient, collapsed+flat) are unit-tested since Dynamic-Type-XXL layout collapse and Increase-Contrast/Reduce-Transparency color flattening are independent and can both be active at once","ColorStoryHeaderView visibly degrades correctly under Dynamic Type XXL and under Increase Contrast/Reduce Transparency"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-021","title":"WidgetSnapshot, publisher, and KitbagWidget extension","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/WidgetSnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/WidgetSnapshotPublisher.swift","KitbagWidget/KitbagWidgetBundle.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingProgressTimelineProvider.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingProgressWidgetView.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001","T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["WidgetSnapshot structurally never contains destination text, verified by a test inspecting its Codable field set","publish is debounced over roughly 1-2 seconds of inactivity before calling WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines","timeline provider output is unit-tested; on-device rendering is explicitly flagged unverifiable in this headless environment via BuildVerificationChecklist, never claimed as working"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-022","title":"Live Activity for an active packing session","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Widgets/PackingSessionActivityAttributes.swift","KitbagWidget/PackingSessionLiveActivity.swift"],"depends_on":["T-021"],"acceptance_criteria":["start/update/end logic is unit-tested against the ActivityKit attributes/content-state shape","on-device rendering is flagged unverifiable in this headless environment, not asserted as working","this is the first feature cut if schedule is squeezed, per the standing cut order"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-023","title":"AddPackingItemIntent and NotificationScheduler","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Intents/AddPackingItemIntent.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Notifications/NotificationScheduler.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["the intent targets the most imminent upcoming trip and has a clean spoken failure path at zero trips, unit-tested","a test proves the core packing flow works identically whether notification permission is granted or denied","notification copy is generic and never contains destination text"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-024","title":"DeepLinkRouter and resolution logic","owner_lane":"services_utilities","files":["Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Navigation/DeepLinkRouter.swift","Kitbag/Sources/ServicesUtilities/Navigation/DeepLinkResolution.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010"],"acceptance_criteria":["resolve() returns .tripNotFound for a deleted or didn't-happen trip id instead of crashing or silently no-opping","a single resolution point prevents simultaneous notification/widget/Siri triggers from racing each other"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-025","title":"Wire deep-link intents and toast fallback into the app root","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Navigation/PendingNavigationIntent.swift"],"depends_on":["T-012","T-021","T-023","T-024"],"acceptance_criteria":["tapping a stale notification/widget/Siri deep link lands safely on Trip List with the shared toast reading that the trip is no longer available","AppRootView.swift (owned by T-002) is only modified here to add intent resolution, not restructured"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-026","title":"Accessibility and empty/loading/error-state audit","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Tests/PrimaryUITests/AccessibilityAuditTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-011","T-012","T-013","T-016","T-017"],"acceptance_criteria":["VoiceOver reads item name, checked state, suggested-vs-custom, and reason/changed status in a sensible order on every screen that has them","no screen distinguishes suggested/custom/locked/review-exception by color alone","delete-trip and mark-didn't-happen confirmations have distinctly different VoiceOver labels and copy weight"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-027","title":"Checklist interaction performance verification","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Tests/ServicesUtilitiesTests/ChecklistPerformanceTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-009","T-012"],"acceptance_criteria":["toggling a single item in a 100+ item checklist is measured to trigger exactly one targeted write, not a full-list re-fetch or re-save"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-028","title":"Final BuildVerificationChecklist sweep","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PolishResilience/Verification/BuildVerificationChecklist.swift"],"depends_on":["T-010","T-014","T-020","T-022","T-025","T-026","T-027"],"acceptance_criteria":["verifiedCoreFlows lists every core workflow that actually has a passing automated test, by name","unverifiableInHeadless lists widget/Live Activity/Siri on-device rendering and live StoreKit purchases explicitly, never marked as working","knownLimitations is populated from whatever this sweep actually finds, not left empty by default"],"status":"pending"}
]}
```

**Quality Gate (Claude) — after round 1**

QUALITY: PASS

## Feedback

This phase output meets the bar. It delivers all four required outputs concretely:

- **Lane-by-lane responsibilities**: data_domain (models, climate data, pure generation engine — zero SwiftUI/StoreKit), services_utilities (repositories, coordinator, entitlements, widgets/intents), primary_ui (screens/stores/components only, never touches ModelContext/StoreKit directly), polish_resilience (scaffolding, error handling, accessibility, final verification sweep) — matches the volatility-based split locked in project_plan.
- **Owned files**: every one of the 28 tasks lists explicit file paths, no vague "the UI layer" hand-waving.
- **Handoffs and forbidden edit zones**: `.pbxproj`/entitlements/scheme/target definitions are frozen after T-001, `AppRootView.swift` is explicitly restricted to extension-only editing by T-025, and integration is scoped to rebase-at-checkpoints rather than continuous merge — this is a real conflict-prevention mechanism, not just a policy statement.
- **tasks-json backlog**: present, well-formed, 28 tasks (T-001–T-028), each with id/title/owner_lane/files/depends_on/acceptance_criteria/status.

Acceptance checks verified:
- **No ownerless tasks**: every task has one of the four valid owner_lane values.
- **Acyclic dependencies**: traced the full graph — T-001 is the only root (no deps), every other task's depends_on points to lower-indexed or otherwise non-cyclic prior work (e.g., T-016→T-018 is a valid forward reference since T-018 depends only on T-009, closing no loop). No cycle exists.
- **Build workers can start immediately**: T-001 (scaffolding) is fully unblocked, and its exit criteria (App Group provably shared, synchronized folder groups mapped to target membership) directly de-risks the exact pitfall named in the reference doc about `.pbxproj`/target-membership mistakes being invisible in a code read.

Sequencing correctly encodes the prior-phase gate structure: the learning-engine go/no-go test (T-007) blocks the coordinator (T-010), which blocks all UI consumption; review-to-learning (T-013/T-014) sits before monetization/insights (T-015–T-018); native surfaces (T-021–T-024) are gated behind T-001's App Group proof and T-010's coordinator, with the standing Live-Activity-first/Siri-second/widget-last cut order preserved in T-022's acceptance criteria. The final BuildVerificationChecklist task (T-028) correctly fans in from every other verification-bearing task and requires `unverifiableInHeadless` to explicitly name widget/Live Activity/Siri rendering and live StoreKit purchases, consistent with every earlier phase's honesty requirement.

No repair needed. This is ready to hand to build workers.

## Coordinator Decision

See the coordinator's message above.

## Final Output

Both engineers converged on the same shape for this phase, but at different zoom levels — Codex sketched a coarse 9-task backlog split roughly by lane and milestone, and Claude took that same structure and drove it all the way down into 28 concrete, immediately-startable tasks with exact file paths, explicit forbidden-edit zones, and a real conflict-prevention mechanism. Nothing in Claude's version contradicts Codex's — it's the same dependency chain from the locked project_plan (scaffolding → data domain → generation engine → coordinator → UI → review-to-learning → monetization/insights → native surfaces → hardening), just made buildable.

The load-bearing decision everyone lands on: `project.pbxproj`, entitlements, scheme, and target definitions are a forbidden edit zone for every lane except the one scaffolding task (T-001) that creates them — after that, lanes only add files under their own folder and Xcode's synchronized folder groups pick them up automatically, so there's never a second hand-edit to the project file. Git is set up with one branch per lane cut from `main` right after scaffolding, with rebase-not-merge integration at defined checkpoints (engine go/no-go, zero-history loop, review-to-learning, final) rather than continuous merging. Ownership splits by volatility exactly as decided in project_plan: data_domain (models, climate data, pure generation engine — zero SwiftUI/StoreKit), services_utilities (repositories, coordinator, entitlements, widgets, intents — the only real seam to the outside world, including the one dual-target `WidgetSnapshot.swift` file), primary_ui (every screen/component, never touches `ModelContext`/StoreKit directly), and polish_resilience (scaffolding itself, error handling, accessibility, and final verification sweep). Nobody starts real UI work until the generation engine's go/no-go test (review demotes an item with a named reason, respecting the 2-data-point evidence rule) actually passes — that's the one gate everything else waits on.

No open disagreement — Codex's list is a strict subset of what Claude's expands into, so I'm carrying Claude's 28-task backlog forward as the final one since it's what lets build workers start immediately, per this phase's acceptance bar.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Lane responsibilities:**
- **data_domain**: SwiftData models + VersionedSchema, bundled climate JSON + ClimateCatalog, pure derivation functions (tripPhase, monthOfMajorityDays, isWithinFreeRetentionWindow), the pure GenerationEngine, LearningHistoryReader, applyGenerationResult. Zero SwiftUI/StoreKit imports anywhere in this lane. Fully unit-testable without a simulator.
- **services_utilities**: Repositories (Trip/GeneratedList/Review), PackingCoordinator (the only bridge between SwiftData and the pure engine), EntitlementStore/StoreKit wiring, WidgetSnapshot + publisher + widget extension + Live Activity, NotificationScheduler, AddPackingItemIntent, DeepLinkRouter, export/share. Owns the one dual-target-membership file (WidgetSnapshot.swift).
- **primary_ui**: Every screen, store, and reusable component — navigation shell, trip create/edit, trip list/detail/checklist, post-trip review, changed-item badge/toast, paywall, settings, insights. Never touches ModelContext or StoreKit directly, only the protocols services_utilities exposes.
- **polish_resilience**: Owns the initial project scaffolding (targets, App Group, StoreKit config, git init, folder groups), AppError, PersistenceBootstrap, the accessibility collapse adapter/HeaderRenderStyle, and the final BuildVerificationChecklist sweep plus cross-cutting accessibility/performance audits.

**Handoffs and forbidden edit zones:**
- `Kitbag.xcodeproj/*`, `.entitlements` files, scheme, and target definitions are forbidden to edit after T-001 lands — new files go under each lane's own synchronized folder, no further pbxproj edits, ever. If a lane needs a new target/top-level folder, that's a stop-and-ask.
- No lane starts real implementation against the coordinator until T-007's generation engine go/no-go test passes.
- `AppRootView.swift` (owned by primary_ui's T-002) may only be extended by T-025 to add deep-link intent resolution, never restructured.
- Integration happens via rebase onto `main` at defined checkpoints (post go/no-go, post zero-history loop, post review-to-learning, final), never continuous merge of half-finished interfaces.

**tasks-json backlog:**

```tasks-json
{"tasks": [
{"id":"T-001","title":"Xcode project scaffolding: targets, App Group, StoreKit config, git init, synchronized folder groups","owner_lane":"polish_resilience","files":["Kitbag.xcodeproj/*","Kitbag/App/KitbagApp.swift","Kitbag/Kitbag.entitlements","KitbagWidget/KitbagWidget.entitlements","Kitbag/Kitbag.storekit",".gitignore","README.md"],"depends_on":[],"acceptance_criteria":["Kitbag.xcodeproj opens and builds an empty shell via xcodebuild","main app target, KitbagWidget extension target, KitbagTests (Swift Testing) and KitbagUITests (XCUITest) targets all exist and compile","App Group container is provably readable and writable from both the main app target and the widget extension target via a throwaway smoke test committed to the repo","Kitbag.storekit contains real product identifiers for monthly/annual premium","git repository initialized with lane branches cut from main after this task lands","Sources/DataDomain, Sources/ServicesUtilities, Sources/PrimaryUI, Sources/PolishResilience folders exist as synchronized groups mapped to correct target membership so later file adds never require pbxproj edits"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-002","title":"Three-tab navigation shell and design tokens","owner_lane":"primary_ui","files":["Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Navigation/TabRoute.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Navigation/AppRootView.swift","Kitbag/Sources/PrimaryUI/Design/DesignTokens.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001"],"acceptance_criteria":["App launches into Trips/Insights/Settings tabs each with an independent NavigationStack","a real kill-and-relaunch resets every tab to its root, no persisted deep-stack restoration","spacing/corner-radius/color-story token values match the locked design_handoff values"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-003","title":"SwiftData models and VersionedSchema","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/Trip.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/PackingItem.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/Review.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/ReviewItemOutcome.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/TripType.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/PackingCategory.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Models/SuggestionReasonKind.swift","[REDACTED:high_entropy].swift"],"depends_on":["T-001"],"acceptance_criteria":["ModelContainer builds successfully from SchemaV1","a unit test creates a Trip with items and a review and confirms cascade-delete removes both when the trip is deleted","didHappen defaults to true and is the only persisted lifecycle fact on Trip"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-004","title":"Schema migration mechanism regression test","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Tests/DataDomainTests/SchemaMigrationSmokeTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003"],"acceptance_criteria":["a standalone test-only two-version schema pair (not SchemaV1) proves ModelContainer migration works on this SDK/Xcode version","test is kept permanently as a regression guard, not deleted after passing once"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-005","title":"Bundled climate dataset and ClimateCatalog","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Resources/ClimateData/climate_normals.json","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Climate/ClimateNormal.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Climate/PrecipitationBand.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Climate/ClimateCatalog.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Climate/RegionCatalogEntry.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Climate/ClimateZoneFallbackEntry.swift"],"depends_on":["T-001"],"acceptance_criteria":["30-40 bundled regions plus fallback climate zones each have all 12 months present","a plausibility-bounds test rejects unrealistic temperature/precipitation values, not just checking coverage","ClimateCatalog loads once in-memory at launch with no per-lookup file IO"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-006","title":"Pure derivation functions: tripPhase, monthOfMajorityDays, isWithinFreeRetentionWindow","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/TripPhase.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/monthOfMajorityDays.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Derivations/isWithinFreeRetentionWindow.swift"],"depends_on":["T-003","T-005"],"acceptance_criteria":["month-boundary tie-break rule is deterministic and unit-tested with a trip that spans exactly two months","isWithinFreeRetentionWindow is relationship-free (takes only ids/dates/counts, never a SwiftData relationship traversal) and unit-tested at the exact 3rd/4th trip boundary","tripPhase is never persisted and is recomputed correctly for every TripPhase case including didNotHappen"],"status":"pending"},
{"id":"T-007","title":"Pure GenerationEngine and the learning go/no-go test","owner_lane":"data_domain","files":["Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationInput.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationResult.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/SuggestionReason.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Generation/GenerationEngine.swift","Kitbag/Sources/DataDomain/Learning/LearningHistorySnapshot.swift","Kitbag/Tests/DataDomainTests/GenerationEngineGoNoGoTests.swift"],"depends_on":["T-005","T-006"],"acceptance_criteria":["generate(input:) is synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated","reviewing trip A with an item marked unused, then generating a same-type-or-region trip B, produces that item visibly demoted with a reason string naming trip A","a single data point never shifts a suggestion; the minimumEvidenceCount=2 rule is explicitly tested","zero-history input still produces a non-empty, sensible list from trip type + climate alone"],"status":"pending"},
```
